title: PolarDB MySQL cross-AZ strong consistency vs MySQL Group Replication
decision_context: Choose a strong-consistency mechanism for MySQL-compatible cross-AZ high availability.

options.name: PolarDB MySQL three-AZ one-primary-one-standby-one-log-node
options.mechanism: Physical Redo log replication plus X-Paxos majority. Primary returns success after Redo reaches majority; log node stores only Redo logs.
strengths: Redo generated during transaction can be transmitted earlier than binlog
log node adds limited storage cost
weaknesses: KB does not provide user-facing setup parameter checklist
preconditions: PolarDB MySQL implementation with consensus_lsn and X-Paxos
fit_scenarios: cross-AZ strong consistency and automatic failover
avoid binlog overhead where binlog is not required
anti_scenarios: teams requiring explicit configuration steps not present in KB

options.name: MySQL Group Replication (MGR)
options.mechanism: Official MySQL group replication; KB notes it requires binlog and has poor behavior for large transactions in this source.
weaknesses: large transactions may perform poorly or fail according to source
fit_scenarios: standard MySQL ecosystem requiring official replication
anti_scenarios: workloads where binlog overhead is unacceptable

recommended_when: Use PolarDB mechanism when the environment is PolarDB MySQL and the target is cross-AZ strong consistency with Redo/X-Paxos semantics.
avoid_when: Avoid inferring general MySQL setup instructions from the PolarDB article; KB does not provide them.
open_questions: What exact PolarDB product settings enable three-AZ mode?
What operational constraints exist for DDL and failback in production?

title: Connection pool sizing trade-off
decision_context: Tune connection pool capacity for high-concurrency Java/database applications.

options.name: Pool too small
options.mechanism: Requests exceed available connections and wait for release.
preconditions: maximumPoolSize or equivalent below workload need
fit_scenarios: strict database connection cap with backpressure

options.name: Pool too large
options.mechanism: Application opens many concurrent database connections.
strengths: can reduce pool wait when database can handle load
preconditions: database has enough CPU, memory, and connection capacity

recommended_when: Size with load tests and database capacity checks; monitor response time and throughput.
avoid_when: Do not use a fixed formula alone as proof of correctness.
open_questions: No HikariCP-specific leakDetectionThreshold details in KB.
